mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.15 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals the onset of a monetary tightening cycle, raising short-term discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the Rank IC is above 0.00 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate jointly indicate a goldilocks expansion where supply capacity and labor demand are both improving, generating positive S&P 500 returns stronger than either signal alone over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or a single-proxy model using only INDPRO achieves higher directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a three-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% foreshadows a weakening residential investment impulse and broader demand deceleration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances during periods when unemployment is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in real retail sales exceeding 1.5% signals an abrupt contraction in consumer demand that immediately pressures corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day return frequency exceeds 50% when RSAFS drops more than 1.5% month-over-month, or the Rank IC is above 0.00 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of real GDP annualized growth below 1% indicate economic stagnation, triggering risk-off repositioning in equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1% for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of low-GDP regimes, or the Rank IC exceeds 0.00 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a federal funds rate below 1% generate sharply negative real interest rates, inflating equity valuations through lower real discount rates and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year window, or returns during the triggered regime are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean via t-test p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing momentum and falling capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day return frequency exceeds 50% when INDPRO declines month-over-month, or the Rank IC is above 0.00 over a 2-year window.
